rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee/eq/const in component type annexes for interface behavior; use implementation annexes only for assign clauses tied to subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible feature names or explicitly defined constants/variables in AGREE expressions; never reference AADL properties directly.
generic_rules: Contracts should express observable interface behavior, not internal architectural details.
Boolean logic in guarantees should reflect required system conditions using named, defined variables, not raw literals.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE blocks must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; and follow strict syntax: assume/guarantee/eq/const/assign.
Equations must declare a named boolean or numeric variable assigned via a valid expression.
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
Avoid using subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without intermediate variables or outputs.
